title: Beetroot, feta and walnut salad
subtitle: Cooked beetroot with crumbled feta, toasted walnuts, and balsamic.
excerpt: Cooked beetroot wedges with crumbled feta, toasted walnuts, and rocket dressed with balsamic vinegar and olive oil. No cooking required if you start with vacuum-packed beetroot. Ready in ten minutes.
sourceNotes: Beetroot and cheese salads appear in British cooking from the Victorian era, when pickled beetroot was a common table accompaniment. The combination of beetroot, walnuts, and a soft salty cheese (feta, goat's cheese, or blue cheese) became a British restaurant and gastropub standard in the 1990s and has since passed into home cooking as vacuum-packed cooked beetroot made the ingredient widely convenient.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Assemble just before serving. The rocket wilts and the feta dissolves if left dressed for more than 30 minutes.

content.text: Vacuum-packed cooked beetroot is the shortcut that makes this a ten-minute recipe. You can roast raw beetroot for a more intense flavour: wrap in foil and roast at 200 °C fan for 40–45 minutes until tender. Either way the salad tastes far better than anything from the supermarket chiller.

Toast the walnuts. This takes three minutes and makes a significant difference: raw walnuts have a slight bitterness that disappears with heat, and the toasted versions add a depth the salad would lack without them.
A large chopping board, a knife, and a small dry frying pan for toasting the walnuts.
Put the walnuts in a dry frying pan over a medium heat. Toast for 2–3 minutes, stirring constantly, until the nuts are golden and fragrant. Tip onto a board and leave to cool, then roughly chop.
Whisk {{balsamic-vinegar}} of balsamic vinegar, {{olive-oil}} of olive oil, and {{honey}} of honey together. Season with {{sea-salt-flakes}} of sea salt and {{black-pepper}} of black pepper.

Arrange the rocket across a wide serving plate or bowl. Add the quartered beetroot, then crumble the feta over the top. Scatter over the toasted walnuts. Drizzle the dressing over everything just before serving. Do not toss — the beetroot will stain the feta and rocket pink if mixed.

items.cause: Salad was assembled too early or tossed before serving.
items.fix: The flavour is unaffected — the pink colour is from beetroot pigment. For next time, add the beetroot and dressing at the last moment and do not toss.

items.cause: Not toasted, or over-toasted and burned.
items.fix: Toast gently over medium heat, watching constantly. Burned walnuts cannot be rescued — discard and start again with fresh.
Add a small splash of red wine vinegar to sharpen it. The honey is there to balance the balsamic but can be reduced if your balsamic is already sweet.
Always add the dressing immediately before serving. Dressed rocket wilts within 20 minutes.

content.text: Beetroot salad in Britain is old: pickled beetroot was a common accompaniment to cold meats long before the gastropub era. The restaurant version of warm roasted beetroot with soft cheese and toasted nuts appeared on British menus in the 1990s and became one of the most widely copied restaurant-to-home dishes of that decade. The version here uses vacuum-packed beetroot and needs no cooking: a salad that is quick to assemble and good enough to make regularly.
